description: This figure consists of one reference {110} pole figure (subplot a) and four experimental {110} pole figures (subplots b-e) characterizing texture evolution of Fe-8%Al bcc ferritic low-density steel after hot torsion deformation. Subplot a displays standard ideal orientations commonly observed in bcc alloy torsion tests, with labeled orientation components: D1 {112̅}<111>, D2 {1̅1̅2}<111>, J1 {01̅1}<2̅11>, F {110}<001>, E1 {011̅}<111>, E2 {01̅1}<111>, J2 {11̅0}<11̅2>. Subplots b-e correspond to deformed samples at true strain ε=2 (max texture intensity 2.50), ε=5 (max 2.46), ε=15 (max 3.43), ε=25 (max 3.17), respectively. The texture intensity first increases with increasing strain to a peak at ε=15, then slightly decreases at ε=25, with dominant orientations consistent with standard bcc torsion texture components. This data supports analysis of dynamic recrystallization texture evolution under hot deformation conditions for the studied alloy.
